data.reasoningText:
The project is missing `ImplicitUsings`. Let me check if implicit usings are disabled. The issue is that `net10.0` projects might need `<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>` or explicit `using System;`. Let me add the missing using directive or enable implicit usings.

Actually, the user asked me to analyze whether subtle logic changes would go undetected by the tests. They didn't ask me to fix compilation issues. But I do need the tests to compile and run to perform a proper analysis.

Let me fix the csproj to enable implicit usings so the tests can run, then I'll analyze the gaps.


I'll take a simpler approach and just add the `using System;` directive to the source file directly rather than modifying the project configuration.

data.content: Good, all 9 tests pass. Now let me perform systematic mutation testing to find which dangerous logic changes survive.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-4247d3c5c0ab4e18a3e06fab378250b6

# Save original
cp AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.orig

# Mutation 1: Editor on system resources gets CanWrite=true (should be false)
echo "=== MUTATION 1: Editor gets Write on system resources ==="
sed 's/Role.Editor => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/Role.Editor => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = true }/' \
  AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.orig > AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs
cd AccessControl.Tests && dotnet test --verbosity quiet 2>&1 | tail -3; cd ..

# Mutation 2: Editor on system resources gets CanRead=false (lose read access)
echo "=== MUTATION 2: Editor loses Read on system resources ==="
sed 's/Role.Editor => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/Role.Editor => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = false, CanWrite = false }/' \
  AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.orig > AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs
cd AccessControl.Tests && dotnet test --verbosity quiet 2>&1 | tail -3; cd ..

# Mutation 3: Guest gets full access (remove deny-all)
echo "=== MUTATION 3: Guest gets CanRead=true, CanWrite=true ==="
sed 's/_ => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = false, CanWrite = false }/_ => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = true }/' \
  AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.orig > AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs
cd AccessControl.Tests && dotnet test --verbosity quiet 2>&1 | tail -3; cd ..

# Mutation 4: User gets Write on normal resources
echo "=== MUTATION 4: User gets Write on normal resources ==="
sed 's/Role.User when !isSystem => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/Role.User when !isSystem => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = true }/' \
  AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.orig > AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs
cd AccessControl.Tests && dotnet test --verbosity quiet 2>&1 | tail -3; cd ..

# Mutation 5: Flip CanPerform ternary (read check becomes write check)
echo "=== MUTATION 5: CanPerform ternary flipped ==="
sed 's/return writeAccess ? perm.CanWrite : perm.CanRead;/return writeAccess ? perm.CanRead : perm.CanWrite;/' \
  AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.orig > AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs
cd AccessControl.Tests && dotnet test --verbosity quiet 2>&1 | tail -3; cd ..

# Mutation 6: Remove null/empty token guard in ElevateRole
echo "=== MUTATION 6: Remove null/empty token guard ==="
sed '/if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(token))/,/return currentRole;/{d}' \
  AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.orig > AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs
cd AccessControl.Tests && dotnet test --verbosity quiet 2>&1 | tail -3; cd ..

# Mutation 7: Guest can elevate to Editor with ELEVATE-EDITOR
echo "=== MUTATION 7: Lower elevation threshold (Guest can become Editor) ==="
sed 's/if (token == "ELEVATE-EDITOR" && currentRole >= Role.User)/if (token == "ELEVATE-EDITOR" \&\& currentRole >= Role.Guest)/' \
  AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.orig > AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs
cd AccessControl.Tests && dotnet test --verbosity quiet 2>&1 | tail -3; cd ..

# Mutation 8: User on system resource gets read access (should be denied)
echo "=== MUTATION 8: User gets Read on system resources ==="
# Change isSystem check for User to always grant read
sed 's/Role.User when !isSystem => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false },/Role.User => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false },/' \
  AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.orig > AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs
cd AccessControl.Tests && dotnet test --verbosity quiet 2>&1 | tail -3; cd ..

# Restore original
cp AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.orig AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs
rm AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs.orig
